title: Parent to Parent Networking - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Parenting & Childcare > Parent to Parent Networking 22 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter African American Community Autism Spectrum Disorder Disabilities Issues Fathers Foster Parents Perinatal Depression Substance Use Disorder Issues ARC LANE COUNTY FAMILIES CONNECTED/FAMILIAS EN CONEXION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer support to parents who are raising a child with an intellectual and/or developmental disability and plan for a positive future. Helps families build community connections and connects families to events, information, and resources. 4181 E Street Springfield, OR 97478 Eligibility: Familes with children 0-21 with intellectual or developmental disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm, some events occur on the weekends Email (541) 343-5256 124 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BABY BLUES CONNECTION PREGNANCY AND POSTPARTUM SUPPORT RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health support for parents and birthing people coping with perinatal mood and anxiety disorders through connection, information and resources. Services include 24-hour phone support, parent to parent support groups online and in-person and partner support for family members who want to support someone coping with postpartum depression or anxiety. Resource packets may be emailed or mailed at no cost. PO Box 33128 Portland, OR 97292 Eligibility: Parents experiencing any kind of perinatal mood disorders and stress related to welcoming a new child, and their family members. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (800) 557-8375 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BLACK PARENT INITIATIVE SAWUBONA Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides culturally specific services and information to promote optimal health. On site food and child care are available for scheduled events. Direct services include: -Parenting support and educational services aimed at mobilizing Black parents to ensure their children achieve educational excellence -Employment services: development, placement, and support -Family Literacy and STEAM fairs -Cultivating Culture: Gardening and healthy eating program -Advocacy for African American families with young children in need of early intervention/early childhood special education -Parent University classes and experiences focused on: Parental engagement in children's education, effective parenting, and all areas of optimal health (intellectual, physical, emotional, spiritual, socio-economic, and relationships) 901 NE Glisan Street Suite 250 Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm. Classes are usually held Thursday nights. Email (503) 284-8000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BRIDGING COMMUNITIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parent-to-parent mentoring, training, workshops, events, parent meetings, virtual meeting spaces, support groups, and advocacy. 1017 N Riverside Avenue Medford, OR 97501 Eligibility: Families with children living with disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (458) 278-0697 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK BEND OFFICE QUARTERLY FAMILY FUN DAYS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers quarterly events geared towards anyone whose life has been affected by disability. Events encourage people to interact and participate in activities such as movie night, picnic in the park, parades, BBQ with the Bend Elks, sensory friendly movies, other sensory friendly events, sledding, and other events. This provides networking opportunities for families. 2525 NE Twin Knolls Drive Suite 7 Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Families and children with disabilities Hours: Varies by event Email (541) 548-8559 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK KLAMATH FALLS OFFICE QUARTERLY FAMILY FUN DAYS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers quarterly events geared towards anyone whose life has been affected by disability. Events encourage people to interact and participate in activities such as movie night, picnic in the park, parades, BBQ with the Bend Elks, sensory friendly movies, other sensory friendly events, sledding, and other events. This provides networking opportunities for families. 2701 Foothills Boulevard Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Families and children with disabilities Hours: Varies by event Email (541) 548-8559 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK WARM SPRINGS OFFICE QUARTERLY FAMILY FUN DAYS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers quarterly events geared towards anyone whose life has been affected by disability. Events encourage people to interact and participate in activities such as movie night, picnic in the park, parades, BBQ with the Bend Elks, sensory friendly movies, other sensory friendly events, sledding, and other events. This provides networking opportunities for families. 1257 Kot-Num Road Warm Springs, OR 97761 Eligibility: Families and children with disabilities Hours: Varies by event Email (541) 548-8559 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EARLY CHILDHOOD HUB OF LANE COUNTY FAMILY RESOURCE CENTERS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Locator tool with list of Family Resource Centers (FRCs) by school district. Provides a range of services and support to families with children, including child literacy groups and story times, playgroups, assistance with accessing school personnel, parent education, parent literacy and computer support, and other drop-in services for families and children. Some locations may offer children's clothing and other supplies. 3171 Gateway Loop Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies by site Email (541) 741-6000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ELYSIUM SANCTUARY GUARDIAN CIRCLE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Resources, outreach, and training connections are provided for those interested in becoming a resource parent, respite provider, or babysitter. Offers virtual support groups focused on sharing and self-care. Confidential Mailing Address Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Not applicable Email Visit Website More Details FACT OREGON FACT FAMILY NETWORK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support to caretakers, parents, siblings or anyone else that has a family member experiencing a disability by providing peer-delivered supports, knowledge, and resources. 2475 SE Ladd Avenue Suite 430 Portland, OR 97214 Eligibility: Caretakers, parents, siblings, or anyone else that has a family member experiencing a disability Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 786-6082 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FOREST GROVE SCHOOL DISTRICT EARLY CHILDHOOD FAMILY RESOURCE CENTER FAMILY RESOURCE MANAGER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides one-on-one information and referrals to families about early learning, parenting support, basic needs, and health. Provides information about and hosts play groups, parenting classes, and early learning workshops. In class, parents have an opportunity to interact with each other and boost their parenting skills while learning nurturing strategies to support their children at home and in school. 200 N 14th Avenue Cornelius, OR 97113 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-6 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (503) 359-8817 5504 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FOSTERCLUB FOSTER PARENT ONLINE TRAINING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The online training is a subscription-based service offering interactive educational modules for credit to resource parents, supportive adults, and child welfare professionals. To confirm if earned credits are accepted, contact your local agency. 620 S Holladay Drive #1 Seaside, OR 97138 Eligibility: Resource parents, supportive adults and/or child welfare professionals Hours: Not applicable Email (503) 717-1552 Get Directions Visit Website More Details IMPACT NW IMPACT NW BRENTWOOD-DARLINGTON COMMUNITY CENTER EARLY CHILDHOOD FATHERING PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Connects fathers with their children, other fathers, and the community through weekly father-child interaction groups and monthly outings designed to increase confidence and skills as fathers and father figures. All services are provided by fathers. 7211 SE 62nd Avenue Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Fathers and father figures with children under age 5 Hours: Hours vary Email (503) 421-0933 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES ABC PARENT MENTOR PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer mentoring to parents impacted by addiction who are involved in ODHS Child Welfare and criminal justice system. Parents are matched with a mentor who helps them stay on track, work through child welfare system and complete substance abuse treatment. Mentors help connect parents to recovery communities. The program is a part of Parents Anonymous Oregon. 7820 NE Holman Street Suite B5 Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Available to any parent who: -Has an open Child Welfare case due to substance abuse -Is currently in treatment for substance abuse -Is reuniting with their children after successful treatment Hours: Message line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 258-4590 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES SALEM PARENT MENTOR PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides peer mentoring to parents impacted by addiction who are involved in ODHS Child Welfare and criminal justice system. Parents are matched with a mentor who helps them stay on track, work through child welfare system and complete substance abuse treatment. Mentors help connect parents to recovery communities. The program is a part of Parents Anonymous Oregon. 3857 Wolverine Street NE Suite 22 Salem, OR 97305 Eligibility: Available to any parent who: -Has an open Child Welfare case due to substance abuse -Is currently in treatment for substance abuse -Is reuniting with their children after successful treatment Hours: Message line: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 258-4685 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK MID-OREGON REGION OFFICE REACH OUT OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a parent warmline offering peer support and connections to local resources, including one-to-one support, resource navigation, support groups, training, and advocacy. 4263 Commercial Street SE Suite 300 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Parents and caregivers raising children and youth with emotional, mental, behavioral, or other health needs Hours: Reach Out Oregon: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm. Closed holidays. Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (833) 732-2467 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK NORTH OFFICE REACH OUT OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a parent warmline offering peer support and connections to local resources, including one-to-one support, resource navigation, support groups, training, and advocacy. 7110 SW Fir Loop Suite 160 Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Parents and caregivers raising children and youth with emotional, mental, behavioral, or other health needs Hours: Reach Out Oregon: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm. Closed holidays. Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (833) 732-2467 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK SOUTH OREGON REACH OUT OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a parent warmline offering peer support and connections to local resources, including one-to-one support, resource navigation, support groups, training, and advocacy. 72A Centennial Loop Suite 150 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Parents and caregivers raising children and youth with emotional, mental, behavioral, or other health needs Hours: Reach Out Oregon: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm. Closed holidays. Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (833) 732-2467 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Operates a call-in center for family members of children and youth with special needs and chronic health conditions (developmental and emotional disabilities, behavioral problems, and chronic physical illnesses) and professionals who serve them. The center specializes in providing information and resources related to health information, health care access, and special needs systems and supports. All calls are confidential. Families may also request to be placed on an email list to receive monthly correspondence about issues of importance to children and youth with special needs in Oregon. 707 SW Gaines Street Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Family members of individuals age 26 and younger with special physical, emotional, behavioral, or mental health needs, including those who are at risk or suspected of having a special need. The child/youth does not have to have a diagnosed condition. Does not require documentation to prove citizenship to receive services. Hours: Message line available 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (833) 990-9930 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON POST ADOPTION RESOURCE CENTER TRAINING CALENDARS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides Oregon adoptive and assisted guardianship families with a schedule of support groups, parenting classes, and educational events 6443 SW Beaverton Hillsdale Highway Suite 205 Portland, OR 97221 Eligibility: ODHS adoptive and assisted guardianship families, or families who have adopted through any state foster care system Hours: Varies by training course Email (503) 241-0799 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST JOHNS SWAPNPLAY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a community space for families to play and connect. With 7 unique indoor and outdoor play zones focused on supporting healthy childhood development, it also offers 2 "swapping" rooms where items like books, toys, gear, and other items can be donated and shared. Other services include parent-child activities, parent networking opportunities, meet-ups, and classes. 7535 N Chicago Avenue Portland, OR 97203 Eligibility: Families with children ages 0-7, older siblings welcome Hours: Monday-Saturday by appointment Email (971) 251-0074 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WILDFLOWER PLAY COLLECTIVE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers an indoor playground. Memberships include daily use of the playspace, weekly classes, events, access to the WildFlower Swap Closet, gently used books, toys, clothes, and gear. Donations are accepted. 58751 S Bachelor Flat Road Saint Helens, OR 97051 Eligibility: Ages 0-6 years old Hours: Saturday-Sunday 7am-7pm Email (503) 664-0669 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ARC LANE COUNTY FAMILIES CONNECTED/FAMILIAS EN CONEXION BABY BLUES CONNECTION PREGNANCY AND POSTPARTUM SUPPORT RESOURCES BLACK PARENT INITIATIVE SAWUBONA BRIDGING COMMUNITIES BRIDGING COMMUNITIES CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK QUARTERLY FAMILY FUN DAYS CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK QUARTERLY FAMILY FUN DAYS CENTRAL OREGON DISABILITY SUPPORT NETWORK QUARTERLY FAMILY FUN DAYS EARLY CHILDHOOD HUB OF LANE COUNTY FAMILY RESOURCE CENTERS FACT OREGON FACT FAMILY NETWORK FOREST GROVE SCHOOL DISTRICT FAMILY RESOURCE MANAGER FOSTERCLUB FOSTER PARENT ONLINE TRAINING IMPACT NW EARLY CHILDHOOD FATHERING PROJECT MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES PARENT MENTOR PROGRAM MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES PARENT MENTOR PROGRAM OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK REACH OUT OREGON OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK REACH OUT OREGON OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK REACH OUT OREGON OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER OREGON POST ADOPTION RESOURCE CENTER TRAINING CALENDARS ST JOHNS SWAPNPLAY ST JOHNS SWAPNPLAY WILDFLOWER PLAY COLLECTIVE WILDFLOWER PLAY COLLECTIVE
